title: True grouped ranking expert trained on lap queries
summary: Current code only rank-blends binary models; official CatBoost and LightGBM ranking objectives support query-group learning directly.
rationale: Current code only rank-blends binary models; official CatBoost and LightGBM ranking objectives support query-group learning directly. Since competition utility depends heavily on ordering cars within the same lap context, a lap-group ranker can learn relative pit urgency better than pointwise logloss alone.
implementation_hint: Build a separate ranker with query groups = (Year,Race,LapNumber). Use CatBoost YetiRank/LambdaMart or LightGBM lambdarank/rank_xendcg, with positive labels from PitNextLap and optional higher gains for strong pit contexts. Evaluate with both global ROC AUC and query-aware diagnostics, then blend the ranker’s OOF scores with the binary stack instead of only query-normalizing existing predictions.
expected_effect: Should improve within-lap separation and provide a cleaner same-lap expert than heuristic query-rank post-processing.
risk: Ranking losses may ignore cross-query calibration and can underperform if most lap groups have very few positives.
